description: Remaining unsold units.
variation_in_prompt: Remaining units
assumption_in_prompt: Unsold% and Supply accurate.

description: Total sold units.
variation_in_prompt: Units sold till date
assumption_in_prompt: Sold% accurate.

description: Average monthly unit absorption derived from annual sales data. Indicates consistent monthly demand and market pace. Critical for forecasting and pipeline planning.
variation_in_prompt: How many units sell per month? Monthly absorption? Average monthly sales? | Tell me the average number of units sold each month.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on last 12-month actual sales divided by 12. Assumes uniform distribution across months. Does not account for seasonality.

description: Time duration required to clear remaining unsold inventory at current absorption pace. Key indicator of market strength and project runway. MOI < 6 months indicates strong demand.
variation_in_prompt: How long to sell remaining? Months of inventory remaining? Project runway? | Tell me how many months it will take to sell all unsold units.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on last 12-month pace (assumed constant going forward). Excludes seasonality and marketing spikes. Absorption steady assumption.

description: Percentage appreciation in unit price from launch to current date. Indicates market value growth and investment returns. Positive growth shows market strength and buyer confidence.
variation_in_prompt: What is price appreciation? % price growth? How much have prices increased? | Tell me the percentage price increase from launch till now.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Launch PSF vs Current PSF comparison. Assumes consistent unit sizes and locations. Market-driven appreciation only.

description: Actual average price per square foot achieved through sales to date. Reflects real market realization vs launch pricing. Important for value assessment and profitability analysis.
variation_in_prompt: What is actual achieved price per sqft? Realized value? Real market PSF? | Tell me the actual price per square foot achieved from sales.
assumption_in_prompt: Calculated from total revenue divided by total saleable area sold. Includes all sold units (booked + completed). Weighted by actual sold quantity.

description: Average revenue generated per sold unit. Reflects market value and pricing strategy. Used for ROI calculations and unit profitability analysis.
variation_in_prompt: Average price per unit? Revenue per unit? Ticket size? | Tell me the average revenue earned per unit sold.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Annual Revenue / Annual Units Sold. Represents average across all unit types and buyers. Includes all revenue (principal only).

description: Absolute number of units sold monthly calculated from velocity percentage. Converts percentage absorption to actionable unit count. Used for inventory and pipeline planning.
variation_in_prompt: How many units sell monthly based on velocity? Monthly sales in units? Monthly absorption units? | Tell me how many units per month are being sold.
assumption_in_prompt: Velocity% × Total Supply = Monthly Units. Applied on Total Supply consistently. Based on last 12-month trend.

description: Estimated market value of remaining unsold units at current market prices. Represents potential future revenue and asset value. Important for balance sheet valuations.
variation_in_prompt: Value of unsold units? Remaining inventory worth? Stock valuation? | Tell me the estimated value of units still available for sale.
assumption_in_prompt: Calculated as Unsold × Unit Size × Current PSF. Assumes current PSF applicable to unsold inventory. PSF and area estimates consistent.

description: Percentage of total supply cleared (sold) annually. Indicates market demand intensity and project success. Used for absorption modeling and market analysis.
variation_in_prompt: What % of supply clears annually? Yearly sell-through rate? Annual absorption %? | Tell me what percentage of total supply is sold each year.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Annual Sales / Total Supply. Represents sell-through percentage. 12-month rolling period.

description: Projected total time to sell entire supply (both sold + remaining) at current absorption pace. Indicates overall project duration from launch to full clearance.
variation_in_prompt: How long to sell entire project? Total project sellout time? Full clearance timeline? | Tell me how long until all units are sold.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Total Supply / Annual Sales pace. Assumes constant demand. Does not account for market changes or external shocks.

description: Ratio of annual sales rate to total supply expressed as percentage. Indicates how efficiently the project is clearing inventory relative to size. Efficiency > 100% is not possible.
variation_in_prompt: Inventory turnover efficiency? Project clearing rate? Absorption efficiency ratio? | Tell me how efficient the project is at selling units.
assumption_in_prompt: Calculation: (Annual Sales × 12) / Supply × 100. Should yield realistic percentage. Data consistency verified.

description: Average current price per unit at today's market rates. Reflects current buyer investment requirement and affordability. Used for customer segmentation and targeting.
variation_in_prompt: Current average unit price? Today's typical unit cost? Current ticket size? | Tell me the average price of units at current market rates.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Unit Size × Current PSF. Assumes current PSF applicable to all remaining units. Size consistency assumed.

description: Average unit price at project launch. Historical reference point for price growth analysis. Shows project positioning at market entry.
variation_in_prompt: Launch period average unit price? Initial pricing? Original ticket size? | Tell me what the average unit price was when the project launched.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Unit Size × Launch PSF. Reflects introductory pricing strategy. Historical reference only.

description: Price difference per square foot between current and launch pricing. Absolute metric of price appreciation. Positive gap indicates market value growth.
variation_in_prompt: PSF price increase? Price per sqft gap? How much has price increased per sqft? | Tell me the price increase per square foot since launch.
assumption_in_prompt: Calculated as Current PSF - Launch PSF. Indicates appreciation per unit area. Market-driven changes captured.

description: Actual achieved price per square foot when accounting for all expenses and revenue. Reflects true value realization in market.
variation_in_prompt: Real achieved PSF? True market realization? Effective pricing? | Tell me the realistic price per square foot that was achieved.
assumption_in_prompt: Derived from (Total Revenue / Total Area of Sold Units). Accounts for all revenue streams and unit mix.

description: Price normalized by unit size ratio. Indicates relative affordability per unit area. Used for cross-project comparisons and market benchmarking.
variation_in_prompt: Price-to-size comparison? Normalized pricing metric? Relative affordability? | Tell me the price-to-size metric for comparison.
assumption_in_prompt: Ratio = Current PSF / Average Unit Size. Abstract metric for comparative analysis. Higher ratio = premium positioning.

description: Time required to completely sell remaining unsold inventory at current monthly absorption pace. Derived from Unsold Units and Monthly Unit Sales. Critical for project timeline and cash flow forecasting.
sample_prompt: How many months to sell remaining inventory?
variation_in_prompt: Time to clear unsold inventory? How long to sell rest of units? Remaining inventory timeline? | Tell me how many months it will take to sell all units left.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Unsold Units / Monthly Units Sold. Assumes constant absorption pace. Does not account for market changes.

description: Percentage of total project units that have completed or are in possession stage. Indicates project completion and delivery progress against total scope.
sample_prompt: What % of units have been delivered so far?
variation_in_prompt: Possession progress? How many units delivered? Completion percentage? | Tell me what percentage of units have been handed over.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on RTO Units / Project Size. Reflects actual possession completion. Updated monthly after possession batches.

description: Percentage of total revenue concentrated from top-selling segment (1BHK, 2BHK, etc.). Indicates market concentration and demand pattern. High concentration = segment-dependent risk.
sample_prompt: What % of revenue comes from top segment?
variation_in_prompt: Revenue concentration by segment? Top segment contribution? Market dependence? | Tell me what percentage of revenue is from the biggest selling unit type.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Max(Segment Revenue) / Total Revenue. Reflects buyer preference concentration. Identifies key demand drivers.

description: Ratio of project absorption to location/market average absorption. Indicates competitive positioning - ratio > 1 means outperforming market, < 1 means underperforming.
sample_prompt: How does project velocity compare to market?
variation_in_prompt: Competitive absorption ratio? vs market average? Market outperformance? | Tell me how this project is selling compared to similar projects.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Project Velocity / Market Average Velocity. Market average calculated from comparable projects in location.
formula_derivation: Project Monthly Velocity / Market Avg Velocity

description: Annualized rate of price appreciation per square foot. Indicates market strength and investment value growth momentum. Helps forecast future market prices.
sample_prompt: What is annual PSF growth rate?
variation_in_prompt: Annual price appreciation rate? Yearly PSF growth? Price momentum? | Tell me how much price per square foot is growing each year.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on (Current PSF - Launch PSF) / (Project Age in Years). Assumes linear growth. Market-driven appreciation.
expected_answer: 8.5% per year, 12.3% per year, 15.6% per year

description: Average number of days inventory remains in stock before sale. Indicates how quickly units move. Lower days = faster turnover = strong demand.
sample_prompt: How many days to sell an average unit?
variation_in_prompt: Unit sales cycle time? Days to sell per unit? Inventory holding period? | Tell me how many days on average a unit stays unsold before being purchased.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on 365 / Clearance Rate. Assumes constant demand distribution. Average metric across all units.

description: Estimated profit per unit sold (Revenue per Unit - Cost per Unit). Indicates project profitability and developer returns per sale.
sample_prompt: What is approximate profit per unit?
variation_in_prompt: Margin per unit? Profit per sale? Developer return per unit? | Tell me the estimated profit or margin made on each unit sold.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on (Revenue per Unit - Total Project Cost / Project Size). Simplified profit calculation. Does not include indirect costs.
formula_derivation: Revenue per Unit - (Total Project Cost / Project Size)

description: Ratio of revenue to total project cost. Indicates project profitability efficiency. Ratio > 1.5 indicates strong margins, < 0.8 indicates challenging economics.
sample_prompt: What is project cost efficiency ratio?
variation_in_prompt: Revenue to cost ratio? Project profitability ratio? Cost effectiveness? | Tell me the ratio of revenue earned to development cost.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Total Revenue / Total Project Cost. Higher ratio = better margins. Reflects project economics strength.

description: Combined timeline until full project completion (remaining sales time + possession time). Indicates total project duration from today to final handover.
sample_prompt: What is total remaining project timeline?
variation_in_prompt: Time till project is fully done? Remaining timeline? Project duration left? | Tell me how many months until this project is completely finished.
assumption_in_prompt: Based on Max(Months to Sell Remaining, Time to Possession). Whichever is longer determines project completion.
formula_derivation: MAX(Months to Sell Remaining, Time to Possession)
